my_thoughts: The reversal works: thinking first (we cannot perceive differently until we think differently), perception = automatic (depends on consciousness state). The student asked causal question (seeking correct sequence), which shows they understand: perception = precondition. They'll likely ask about categorical definition next.
accumulated_insight: T2: Student asked causal question ('Before we can think differently, is it necessary to perceive things differently?')—seeking correct sequence via asks for causal precondition and proposes external driver. They understand: perception = precondition (external driver). The subject_object_inversion mechanism reverses: thinking first (we cannot perceive differently until we think differently), perception = automatic (depends on consciousness state) ('We cannot perceive differently until we think differently. We have control only of our thoughts; we have no control over perception. Perception depends particularly on the state of consciousness'). Pattern: incapacity admission → practical protocol → causal question → reversal → [next: categorical question]. They're grasping: thinking = controlled, perception = automatic (reversal).
visible_response:
We cannot perceive differently until we think differently. We have control only of
our thoughts; we have no control over perception.
Perception depends particularly on
the state of consciousness. If one awakes for sufficiently long, one can perceive many
things one does not perceive now.
It does not depend on desire or decision.
